content.problemStatement:
Given a parameter KKK, we define a recursive process on an array AAA of size MMM as follows:


If M=1M = 1M=1, return AAA.
Choose an index iii such that 1≤i≤min⁡(M−1,K)1 \leq i \leq \min(M - 1, K)1≤i≤min(M−1,K).
Let P=A[1,i]P = A[1,i]P=A[1,i] and Q=A[(i+1),M]Q = A[(i + 1),M]Q=A[(i+1),M]. Repeat the process from step 111 for the arrays PPP and QQQ to get P1P_1P1​ and Q1Q_1Q1​.
Concatenate P1P_1P1​ and Q1Q_1Q1​ in any order to get A1A_1A1​, i.e., either place all elements of P1P_1P1​ before Q1Q_1Q1​ or after.
Return A1A_1A1​.
Count the number of permutations of length NNN that can be sorted using the above process. Since the answer can be huge, print it modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7.

Note that A[L,R]A[L, R]A[L,R] denotes the subarray [AL,AL+1,…AR][A_L, A_{L+1}, \ldots A_R][AL​,AL+1​,…AR​].


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists two space-separated integers NNN and KKK - length of the permutation and the parameter.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, the count of sortable permutations modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 500050005000.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111 : Both the permutations of size 222 are sortable.
Test case 222 : The permutations [1,2,3][1, 2, 3][1,2,3], [1,3,2][1, 3, 2][1,3,2], [3,1,2][3, 1, 2][3,1,2] and [3,2,1][3, 2, 1][3,2,1] are sortable using the process.
For example, we can do the following steps to sort [3,2,1][3, 2, 1][3,2,1] :

Pick i=1i = 1i=1, and then we need to perform the recursive process on P=[3]P = [3]P=[3] and Q=[2,1]Q = [2, 1]Q=[2,1]

[3][3][3] will be returned as is, since its size is 111.
For [2,1][2, 1][2,1], we will again choose i=1i = 1i=1 and split into [2][2][2] and [1][1][1], both of which will be returned as is because their sizes are 111.

Concatenate them in reverse order to get [1,2][1, 2][1,2]




Thus, after performing the recursive process we have P1=[3]P_1 = [3]P1​=[3], Q1=[1,2]Q_1 = [1, 2]Q1​=[1,2]
Concatenate them in reverse order, Q1+P1Q_1 + P_1Q1​+P1​ to get A1=[1,2,3]A_1 = [1, 2, 3]A1​=[1,2,3] which is sorted.
